rulings.Case Title: SHERMAN FERGUSON et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 23, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Rulings: Line 2. Defendant F.P. Lathrop Construction Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Peterson was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories and Mr. Cantley¿s deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs dismissed their strict liability cause of action and claim for punitive damages, which were the only issues defendant sought to have summarily adjudicated. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID WILSON et al VS. FOSTER WHEELER LLC (FKA FOSTER WHEELER CORPORATION)
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 23, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Underground Construction Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Although this motion appears on the court¿s calendar, the court has no courtesy copy of the motion and a review of Lexis and the court¿s database shows that no such motion was ever filed. Although the court¿s database shows a motion being filed by defendant on June 8, 2012, a review of the actual document that was filed was a motion by another defendant. = (503/TLJ)
